cards.title: Message Queues
cards.description: Async messaging, delivery semantics, backpressure, partitioning

cards.title: DNS
cards.description: Domain resolution, record types, caching, propagation

cards.title: Load Balancer
cards.description: Traffic distribution, algorithms, health checks, L4 vs L7

cards.title: CDN
cards.description: Edge caching, cache invalidation, origin offload

cards.title: Caching
cards.description: In-memory caching, eviction policies, distributed cache patterns

cards.title: Databases
cards.description: SQL vs NoSQL, indexing, replication, consistency models

cards.title: API Gateway
cards.description: Routing, auth, rate limiting, request transformation

cards.title: Rate Limiter
cards.description: Throttling strategies, distributed rate limiting

cards.title: Proxies
cards.description: Forward vs reverse proxy, use cases, TLS termination

cards.title: Service Discovery
cards.description: Client-side vs server-side, health registration, DNS-based

cards.title: WebSockets / SSE / Long Polling
cards.description: Real-time communication patterns, trade-offs

cards.title: Blob / Object Storage
cards.description: Binary storage, chunking, multipart upload, durability

cards.title: Distributed File System
cards.description: Block storage, metadata management, fault tolerance

cards.title: Search
cards.description: Inverted index, relevance ranking, distributed search

cards.title: Authentication
cards.description: Session vs token auth, OAuth, OIDC, MFA, service-to-service

cards.title: Consistent Hashing
cards.description: Ring-based key distribution, virtual nodes, rebalancing

cards.title: Bloom Filter
cards.description: Probabilistic set membership, false positives, space trade-offs

cards.title: CAP Theorem
cards.description: Consistency, availability, partition tolerance trade-offs

cards.title: ACID vs BASE
cards.description: Transaction guarantees, eventual consistency

cards.title: Replication Strategies
cards.description: Leader-follower, multi-leader, leaderless

cards.title: Sharding Strategies
cards.description: Range, hash, directory-based sharding

cards.title: Rate Limiting Algorithms
cards.description: Token bucket, leaky bucket, sliding window

cards.title: Circuit Breaker
cards.description: Failure detection, states, fallback strategies

cards.title: Consensus (Raft / Paxos)
cards.description: Leader election, log replication, split-brain prevention

cards.title: Saga Pattern
cards.description: Distributed transactions, choreography vs orchestration

cards.title: URL Shortener
cards.description: Hashing, redirects, analytics, scaling reads

cards.title: Twitter / News Feed
cards.description: Fan-out, timeline generation, celebrity problem

cards.title: WhatsApp / Chat System
cards.description: WebSockets, message ordering, delivery guarantees

cards.title: YouTube / Video Streaming
cards.description: Chunked upload, transcoding, CDN, adaptive bitrate

cards.title: Uber / Ride Sharing
cards.description: Geo-indexing, real-time matching, surge pricing

cards.title: Google Drive / File Storage
cards.description: Chunking, deduplication, sync conflict resolution

cards.title: Web Crawler
cards.description: BFS/DFS, politeness, deduplication, scheduling

cards.title: Search Autocomplete
cards.description: Trie, top-k, personalization, latency

cards.title: Notification System
cards.description: Push/pull, fan-out, delivery guarantees, retries

cards.title: Ticketmaster / Booking
cards.description: Inventory locking, concurrency, seat reservation

cards.title: Distributed Cache
cards.description: Consistent hashing, eviction, replication, thundering herd

cards.title: Payment System
cards.description: Idempotency, exactly-once, ledger design, fraud detection

cards.title: Distributed ID Generator
cards.description: Snowflake IDs, clock skew, monotonicity

cards.title: Key-Value Store
cards.description: LSM tree, compaction, replication, consistency

cards.title: Array
cards.description: Contiguous block, O(1) indexed access, O(n) middle insert/delete. The foundation every other structure builds on.

cards.title: Dynamic Array
cards.description: Growable array that doubles when full. Amortized O(1) append - the canonical amortization argument.

cards.title: Circular Buffer
cards.description: Fixed-size ring with head/tail wraparound. True O(1) enqueue/dequeue, no resize - fixed-capacity FIFO and sliding windows.

cards.title: Heap
cards.description: Binary tree where every parent beats its children, stored in a flat array. O(1) peek, O(log n) push/pop - the priority queue and "top-K" workhorse.

cards.title: String
cards.description: Immutable sequence of characters. Indexing, slicing, and the building/scanning patterns that dominate text problems.

cards.title: Linked List
cards.description: Nodes chained by pointers. O(1) insert/delete at a known node, O(n) search - the pointer-manipulation classic.

cards.title: Stack
cards.description: LIFO. O(1) push/pop - function calls, matching, undo, and the monotonic-stack trick.

cards.title: Queue
cards.description: FIFO. O(1) enqueue/dequeue - BFS frontier, scheduling, and the deque generalization.

cards.title: Deque
cards.description: Double-ended queue. O(1) add/remove at both ends - generalizes stack + queue; engine of sliding-window max/min and 0/1-BFS.

cards.title: Hash Table
cards.description: Key→value via a hash function. Average O(1) insert/lookup/delete - the workhorse map; chaining vs open addressing, load factor, resize.

cards.title: Hash Set
cards.description: Membership-only hash structure. Average O(1) contains/add - dedup and seen-set patterns.

cards.title: LRU Cache
cards.description: Hash map welded to a doubly linked list: O(1) get/put with least-recently-used eviction. The canonical "design a cache" interview build.

cards.title: LFU Cache
cards.description: Frequency buckets of LRU lists + a min-freq pointer: O(1) get/put evicting the least-frequently-used key. LRU with a popularity axis.

cards.title: Binary Tree
cards.description: Each node has up to two children. The traversal orderings and the base for BST, heap, and trie.

cards.title: Binary Search Tree (BST)
cards.description: Ordered tree: left < node < right. O(log n) search/insert/delete when balanced, O(n) when skewed.

cards.title: Trie
cards.description: Prefix tree keyed by character path. O(L) lookup by length, not by count - autocomplete and prefix queries.

cards.title: Balanced BST
cards.description: **Hub** - survey + decision layer for self-balancing BSTs: what balancing buys, rotations, and which scheme when. Routes to AVL / Red-Black / B-tree.

cards.title: Skip List
cards.description: Sorted linked list with randomized express-lane towers - expected O(log n) search/insert/delete, no rotations. Redis sorted sets' backbone.

cards.title: AVL Tree
cards.description: Strictly height-balanced BST (subtree heights differ ≤ 1). Tighter balance → faster lookups, more rotations on writes. Read-heavy workloads.

cards.title: Red-Black Tree
cards.description: Loosely balanced via color invariants; recolor-first, rotate-rarely. Fewer write rotations - the library default (`std::map`, `TreeMap`, kernel).

cards.title: B-Tree
cards.description: High-fan-out balanced tree, many keys per node, block-aligned to minimize disk seeks. The structure behind database and filesystem indexes.

cards.title: Segment Tree
cards.description: Tree over array ranges. O(log n) range query + point/range update - the CP range-aggregate workhorse.

cards.title: Fenwick Tree (BIT)
cards.description: Array where index `i` owns a range sized by its lowest set bit. O(log n) point update + prefix sum - segment tree's lighter sum-only sibling.

cards.title: Union-Find (DSU)
cards.description: Disjoint-set forest with path compression + union by rank. Near-O(1) connectivity - Kruskal's MST, cycle detection, grouping.

cards.title: Graph
cards.description: Nodes + edges. Adjacency list vs matrix, directed/weighted variants - the substrate every traversal algorithm walks.

cards.title: Bloom Filter
cards.description: Probabilistic membership structure - never false negatives, tunable false-positive rate. O(k) insert/lookup, O(m) bits for m bits and k hash functions.

cards.title: B-Plus Tree
cards.description: B-tree variant with all values at leaves, internal nodes as pure routing keys, leaves linked for range scans - the structure behind MySQL InnoDB and PostgreSQL indexes.

cards.title: Interval Tree
cards.description: Augmented BST storing intervals; O(log n + k) stabbing and overlap queries - the structure for sweep-line and scheduling problems.

cards.title: Suffix Array
cards.description: Sorted array of suffix indices. O(n log² n) prefix-doubling build, O(m log n) pattern search - lighter than a suffix tree; the CP string and genomics workhorse.

cards.title: Binary Search
cards.description: Halve a sorted search space each step - O(log n). Generalizes to any monotonic predicate, the basis of "binary search on the answer".

cards.title: Sorting
cards.description: Comparison sorts bottom out at O(n log n) (merge/quick/heap); counting & radix break it to O(n) on bounded keys. Stability, the lower bound.

cards.title: Merge Sort
cards.description: Divide in half, sort each, merge. Guaranteed O(n log n) in all cases and stable, at O(n) space - the safe choice and the basis of external sort.

cards.title: Quicksort
cards.description: Partition around a pivot in place, recurse on each side. O(n log n) average with the smallest constant; O(n²) tail unless randomized. Holds quickselect.

cards.title: Insertion Sort
cards.description: Grow a sorted prefix, sliding each new element into place. O(n²) worst but O(n) on nearly-sorted data; stable, in-place, online - Timsort's small-run sort.

cards.title: Counting Sort
cards.description: Tally each key, emit in key order. O(n + k), beating the comparison bound by not comparing - integer keys in a small range only. Radix's inner pass.

cards.title: Radix Sort
cards.description: Stable counting sort per digit, least-significant first. O(d·(n + b)) - linear for fixed-width keys, where counting sort's range would explode.

cards.title: Heapsort
cards.description: Build a max-heap, repeatedly extract the max into a sorted suffix. The only comparison sort with both worst-case O(n log n) and O(1) space; not stable.

cards.title: Selection Sort
cards.description: Repeatedly pick the min of the unsorted suffix. O(n²) always, minimal swaps - the simplest sort, mostly a teaching baseline.

cards.title: Bucket Sort
cards.description: Scatter into buckets by value range, sort each, concatenate. O(n) average on uniform data - the distribution sort for floats in a range.

cards.title: Quickselect
cards.description: Quicksort's partition without recursing on both sides. O(n) average to find the k-th smallest - selection without full sort.

cards.title: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
cards.description: Explore level by level with a queue. Shortest path in unweighted graphs, O(V + E).

cards.title: Depth-First Search (DFS)
cards.description: Explore as deep as possible, backtrack. O(V + E) - cycle detection, components, topological order.

cards.title: Topological Sort
cards.description: Linear order of a DAG respecting edges. Kahn's BFS or DFS post-order - dependency resolution, build order.

cards.title: Lowest Common Ancestor (LCA)
cards.description: Deepest shared ancestor of two tree nodes. Binary lifting: O(n log n) preprocess, O(log n) per query - the many-queries-on-a-static-tree workhorse.

cards.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
cards.description: Shortest paths from a source with non-negative weights via a priority queue. O((V + E) log V).

cards.title: Bellman-Ford
cards.description: Shortest paths that tolerate negative edges and detect negative cycles. O(V·E) - slower than Dijkstra, more general.

cards.title: Floyd-Warshall
cards.description: All-pairs shortest paths by DP over intermediates. O(V³) - dense graphs, small V, transitive closure.

cards.title: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal / Prim)
cards.description: Cheapest tree connecting all nodes. Kruskal (sort + DSU) or Prim (PQ) - network design.

cards.title: Recursion
cards.description: A function defined in terms of itself: base case + recursive case. The substrate under divide-and-conquer, backtracking, and DP.

cards.title: Divide and Conquer
cards.description: Split into independent subproblems, solve each recursively, combine. The paradigm behind merge sort, Karatsuba, and closest pair - O(n log n) via the Master Theorem.

cards.title: Backtracking
cards.description: Build candidates incrementally, abandon dead ends (prune). The systematic search for subsets, permutations, and constraint problems.

cards.title: Dynamic Programming
cards.description: Solve overlapping subproblems once, reuse via memo or table. Optimal substructure → the recurrence that collapses exponential to polynomial.

cards.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
cards.description: O(n²) DP comparing every pair, or O(n log n) patience sorting via a binary-searched `tails` array - same answer, different state representation.

cards.title: Longest Common Subsequence
cards.description: Compare two strings with a 2D `dp[i][j]` table - match extends the diagonal, mismatch takes the max of dropping a char from either side. O(n·m) time/space, the basis of `diff`, edit distance, and DNA alignment.

cards.title: Bit Manipulation
cards.description: Operate on individual bits: masks, shifts, XOR tricks. O(1) set operations and the bitmask-DP enabler.

cards.title: Greedy
cards.description: Take the locally best choice each step; prove it's globally optimal by an exchange argument. Interval scheduling, Huffman, MST choices.

cards.title: String Matching (KMP)
cards.description: Find a pattern in a text in O(n + m) via the failure function - slide the pattern on a mismatch without ever rewinding the text. The substring-search workhorse.

cards.title: Z-Algorithm
cards.description: Computes, for each position, the longest prefix starting there (the Z-array) in O(n) by reusing a sliding match window. KMP's twin; pattern search via `P$T`.

cards.title: Manacher Algorithm
cards.description: Finds the longest palindromic substring in O(n) by mirroring already-computed radii across a known palindrome's center - collapses O(n²) expand-around-center to linear.

cards.title: Aho-Corasick
cards.description: KMP's failure function generalized to a trie - build once over all patterns, scan the text once, O(n + Σm + matches) deterministic multi-pattern matching.

cards.title: Number Theory
cards.description: **Hub** - survey + decision layer for the contest math toolkit: GCD, modular exponentiation, the sieve. Routes to each member; ties them together via mod-prime arithmetic.

cards.title: Modular Arithmetic
cards.description: Arithmetic under a modulus: add/mul/pow in O(log n), modular inverse, the Chinese Remainder Theorem - the foundation of every contest problem that says "answer mod 10⁹+7".

cards.title: Modular Exponentiation
cards.description: Compute base^exp mod m in O(log exp) by squaring instead of multiplying exp times - the engine behind RSA, Fermat's modular inverse, and matrix exponentiation for Fibonacci.

cards.title: Euclidean GCD
cards.description: `gcd(a,b) = gcd(b, a mod b)` in O(log min(a,b)) - the extended version recovers Bézout's coefficients, giving a modular inverse for any modulus, not just primes.

cards.title: Sieve of Eratosthenes
cards.description: Mark every composite up to n in O(n log log n) by striking multiples of each prime from p² on. Bulk primality/factorization - linear-sieve and segmented-sieve variants.

cards.title: Strongly Connected Components
cards.description: Find all maximal groups where every node can reach every other. Kosaraju (two-pass DFS + transpose) or Tarjan (single-pass, low-link values) - both O(V + E). Foundation of 2-SAT and condensation DAG analysis.

cards.title: Maximum Flow
cards.description: **[Hub]** Survey + decision layer for max-flow algorithms: Ford-Fulkerson, Edmonds-Karp, Dinic, and the bipartite-matching reduction.

cards.title: Ford-Fulkerson
cards.description: Augment flow along any path in the residual graph. O(E·\

cards.title: Edmonds-Karp
cards.description: Ford-Fulkerson with BFS for shortest augmenting paths. O(VE²) - polynomial regardless of capacity values.

cards.title: Dinic
cards.description: BFS level graph + DFS blocking flow with current-arc optimization. O(V²E) general, O(E√V) on unit-capacity graphs - the fast max-flow choice.

cards.title: Bipartite Matching
cards.description: Maximum set of non-overlapping pairs in a bipartite graph via augmenting paths - Kuhn's O(VE) or Hopcroft-Karp O(E√V). Job/task assignment, König's theorem for min vertex cover.

cards.title: Rabin-Karp
cards.description: Rolling polynomial hash over a sliding window. O(n + m) average, O(nm) worst case - the primary choice for multi-pattern search and substring fingerprinting.

cards.title: String Hashing
cards.description: Prefix-hash a string once in O(n), then compare any two substrings in O(1) - the general polynomial-hash toolkit behind Rabin-Karp, double hashing, and hash + binary-search-on-answer.

cards.title: Kadane
cards.description: Maximum subarray sum in O(n) via a single pass - extend the running subarray or restart, whichever is larger. The canonical space-compressed DP; extends to circular arrays and 2-D max-sum rectangle.

cards.title: Two Pointers
cards.description: Two indices walking a sequence (same or opposite ends). Pair-sum, dedup, partition - O(n) where brute force is O(n²).

cards.title: Sliding Window
cards.description: A moving sub-range over a sequence: expand right, contract left on a constraint. Longest/shortest contiguous subarray in O(n).

cards.title: Prefix Sum
cards.description: Precompute cumulative sums for O(1) range queries. The "subarray sum equals K" and difference-array family.

cards.title: Difference Array
cards.description: Two O(1) point writes per range update, one prefix-sum pass to reconstruct - O(n + q) vs O(n·q) naive. Batch range-increment problems.

cards.title: Fast & Slow Pointers
cards.description: Two pointers at different speeds. Cycle detection, middle of a list, cycle start - Floyd's tortoise and hare.

cards.title: Merge Intervals
cards.description: Sort by start, sweep, merge overlaps. The interval-overlap, meeting-rooms, insert-interval family.

cards.title: Cyclic Sort
cards.description: Place each value at its index in O(n) when values are 1..n. Finds the missing/duplicate number without extra space.

cards.title: Monotonic Stack
cards.description: A stack kept increasing or decreasing. Next-greater-element, daily temperatures, histogram - amortized O(n).

cards.title: Monotonic Queue
cards.description: A deque kept increasing or decreasing. Sliding-window max/min in O(n) - the two-ended sibling of monotonic stack.

cards.title: Top-K Elements
cards.description: A size-k heap over a stream. K largest/smallest/most-frequent in O(n log k) without sorting everything.

cards.title: Binary Search on Answer
cards.description: Binary search the answer space when feasibility is monotonic. "Minimize the max" / "maximize the min" problems.

cards.title: Modified Binary Search
cards.description: Binary search on a transformed array (rotated, peaked, partially sorted) - same halving, different monotonic signal at `mid`. Rotated search, peak element, first/last position.

cards.title: Subsets & Permutations
cards.description: Backtracking templates for the power set, permutations, and combinations - with dedup for repeats.

cards.title: Backtracking
cards.description: Choose / explore / un-choose over a decision tree, pruning dead branches - the recognition + transfer layer for constraint-satisfaction search.

cards.title: Tree & Graph Traversal
cards.description: The BFS/DFS skeletons applied as a problem pattern: level-order, path-sum, connected components, flood fill.

cards.title: DP Patterns
cards.description: The recurring DP shapes: 0/1 knapsack, unbounded, LIS, LCS, grid paths, interval DP - recognition and the state to pick.

cards.title: Pattern Selection Cheat Sheet
cards.description: The aggregator: trigger phrase → which pattern. The fast lookup that ships last, once every pattern exists.

cards.title: Two Heaps
cards.description: A max-heap of the lower half + min-heap of the upper half. Median of a stream and sliding-window median in O(log n) per element.

cards.title: K-Way Merge
cards.description: Min-heap of k sorted sequence heads - pop the global minimum, push its successor. O(N log k) to merge N total elements across k sorted lists.

cards.title: Interval DP
cards.description: State is `dp[i][j]` for subrange `[i,j]`; fill by interval length, try every split point k. O(n³) - matrix chain, burst balloons, palindrome cost.

cards.title: In-place Reversal of a Linked List
cards.description: Rewire next pointers with three variables (prev, curr, next). Reverse a list, reverse k-groups, palindrome check - O(n) time, O(1) space.

cards.title: Matrix Traversal
cards.description: BFS/DFS on a 2D grid with direction vectors. Island count, flood fill, shortest path in grid - the implicit-graph pattern.

cards.title: State Machine DP
cards.description: Finite named states with explicit allowed transitions, DP over (index, state). Stock cooldown, k-transactions, paint-house - O(n · S) time, O(S) space with rolling arrays.

cards.title: Bitmask DP
cards.description: Encode a subset of n ≤ 20 items as an integer bitmask, DP over all 2ⁿ subsets. TSP, optimal assignment, minimum coverage - O(2ⁿ · n) or O(2ⁿ · n²).

cards.title: Frequency Array
cards.description: Array indexed by value: O(1) increment/lookup for bounded integer or character keys. Replaces a hash map when the key range fits - anagram detection, counting sort, sliding-window distribution matching.

cards.title: Graph Coloring
cards.description: Assign colors to nodes so no two adjacent nodes match. 2-coloring (bipartite check) in O(V + E); k-coloring for k ≥ 3 is NP-complete - backtracking for small n, bitmask DP for n ≤ 20.

cards.title: Meet in the Middle
cards.description: Split an exponential search space in half, enumerate each independently (2^(n/2) each), sort one, binary-search from the other. Turns O(2ⁿ) into O(2^(n/2) · n) - the go-to for n ≤ 40 subset-sum problems.
